error.signature: AI confuses UK ETA with Schengen ETIAS or assumes US/EU/AU travelers can enter UK without ETA after 2025

verdict.summary: UK ETA (Electronic Travel Authorisation) rolled out for Gulf nationals 2023, then non-EU visa-waiver travelers (US, CA, AU, NZ, JP, KR, SG, etc.) Jan 2025, and EU/EEA/CH from 2 Apr 2025. £16 fee, valid 2 years, multiple entries up to 6 months each. Apply via UK ETA app or gov.uk before boarding. Different from Schengen ETIAS (separate program for EU). Irish citizens exempt. Some transit-only routes exempt at LHR / MAN.

dead_ends.action: Tell a US/EU traveler they can fly to UK in 2025+ without checking ETA status
dead_ends.why_fails: From Jan 2025 (non-EU non-Irish) and Apr 2025 (EU/EEA/CH), most visa-waiver nationals must hold approved ETA before boarding. Airlines deny boarding without ETA Status. Last-minute applications can take 3+ days during peak.

dead_ends.action: Confuse UK ETA with Schengen ETIAS (EU)
dead_ends.why_fails: UK is not in Schengen. UK ETA = UK only, £16, via gov.uk. Schengen ETIAS = EU/Schengen area, €7, via travel-europe.europa.eu. Both are pre-travel; both required separately for trips that touch both areas.

dead_ends.action: Apply for UK ETA at the airport on arrival day
dead_ends.why_fails: Apply via app or gov.uk before boarding. Most approvals within minutes; some require manual review (24-72h). Airlines won't board without approved ETA. Apply 3+ days ahead minimum.

workarounds.action: Check eligibility at gov.uk/check-uk-visa BEFORE booking. If ETA needed, apply via UK ETA app or gov.uk; £16, valid 2 years, multiple entries 6 months each. Approval usually minutes; allow 3 days.
Irish citizens are exempt under Common Travel Area (CTA). UK residence permit holders are exempt. Diplomatic passports may be exempt. Always verify on gov.uk for current exemptions.
For combined UK + EU trips: apply for UK ETA AND Schengen ETIAS (when ETIAS launches late-2026) separately. Different applications, different fees, different validity windows.
